title: Acceptance criteria — pi.dev port (v1.0)
scope: Behaviour impacted by the @opencode-ai/sdk → @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent migration, including the legacy-config wipe, the rebuilt onboarding flow, the unified review engine (basic + agentic), the pi tools extension, and the doctor/setup CLI surface.

use_cases.title: Legacy config detection and clean-break wipe
use_cases.description: User upgrades to v1.0 with an old-schema config on disk. The CLI must refuse to run normal commands until the user has explicitly typed 'wipe' (or passed --migrate-yes / KODE_REVIEW_MIGRATE_YES=1). The migration tears down the old indexer Docker project, removes the watch-config dir, and resets the main config to fresh defaults — in that order.
acceptance_criteria: needsMigration() returns true iff the on-disk config has the legacy `provider` key and false otherwise.
runMigration() is a no-op when no legacy schema is detected (returns { performed: false, skipReason: 'no-old-schema' }) — does NOT reset config or call docker.
Typed confirmation must equal exactly 'wipe' (case-sensitive). Any other input aborts with skipReason: 'aborted' and leaves config + docker untouched.
--migrate-yes flag and KODE_REVIEW_MIGRATE_YES=1 env var both bypass the prompt.
When stdin is not a TTY and no readLine override is given, runMigration aborts with skipReason: 'no-tty' rather than hanging.
composeProject is read from the legacy config BEFORE resetConfig() wipes it (regression guard for the previous-name tear-down).
Docker tear-down uses `docker compose -p <legacy-project-name> down -v` (note `-v` to wipe volumes).
If `docker` is not on PATH, migration skips the docker step but still wipes config + watch dir.
If docker tear-down exits non-zero, migration logs and continues — config wipe still happens.
rm() is invoked on the watch-config directory (path contains `kode-review-watch`), not the main config path.
After resetConfig() the on-disk config has the new schema (no `provider`, `model`, `variant`, or `antigravity` fields).
Top-level main() in src/index.ts gates all commands except --doctor on `!needsMigration()` — running any other command on a legacy config exits non-zero with a migration message.

use_cases.title: Onboarding wizard requires pi prerequisites
use_cases.description: First-run flow checks pi installed, pi has at least one usable model, and a VCS CLI is available. If pi prerequisites fail the wizard does NOT mark onboarding complete and the post-wizard 'run a review now?' prompt is suppressed.
acceptance_criteria: isPiInstalled() returns true iff `pi` is on PATH (commandExists).
piHasUsableModel() returns false when `pi --list-models` exits non-zero, when stdout matches /No models available/i, or when stdout is empty/whitespace; true otherwise.
Wizard returns false when pi is missing — caller must NOT proceed to ask 'run a review now?'.
Wizard returns false when pi has no usable model — caller must NOT proceed.
Wizard returns true only when pi installed AND model present AND VCS step completed; only then is setOnboardingComplete(true) called.
src/index.ts: when wizard returns false, the function exits cleanly without prompting for or attempting a review.
Wizard text directs the user to run `pi /login` (not a built-in OAuth flow) — auth is delegated to pi.

use_cases.title: Basic review (runReview) — no tools
use_cases.description: Non-agentic review path: hand the diff + prompt to pi, disable ALL tools, return the assistant's final text.
acceptance_criteria: createAgentSession is called with noTools: 'all' (no built-ins, no extensions).

Model selection cascade — resolveModel tries, in order: (1) --model pattern, (2) KODE_REVIEW_MODEL env var, (3) pi's defaultProvider/defaultModel (project settings override global), (4) first available model from ModelRegistry.getAvailable(). The first match in the registry wins.
When --model matches `provider/id`, that exact model is selected and passed to createAgentSession.
When --model is provided but matches no available model, runReview throws with a message containing 'not available in pi' (hard error — no fallback for explicit --model).
When KODE_REVIEW_MODEL points at an unavailable model, runReview logs a warning and falls through to the pi default (then first-available); it does NOT throw.
When pi's defaultModel points at an unavailable model, runReview logs a warning and falls through to first-available; it does NOT throw.
readPiDefaultPattern treats (defaultProvider, defaultModel) as a pair per scope — a project that overrides only defaultModel must NOT be combined with the global defaultProvider (no cross-pairing).
When ModelRegistry returns an empty list, runReview throws an error containing 'No pi provider has usable credentials' (NO_PI_AUTH-style).
On agent_end, runReview returns the text of the final assistant message (extractReviewContent walks backwards to the last assistant message).
session.dispose() is called exactly once whether the prompt succeeds, throws, or times out (verified via finally).
session.prompt() rejection is surfaced to the caller AND session is still disposed (no leak).

use_cases.title: Agentic review (runAgenticReview) — extensions enabled
use_cases.description: Agentic path enables extension tools (read_file + indexer tools) but keeps pi's built-ins disabled. Iteration count and truncation flag are surfaced.
acceptance_criteria: createAgentSession is called with noTools: 'builtin' (NOT 'all') — extension tools must remain enabled.
Each tool_execution_end event increments toolCallCount; tool_execution_start events do NOT increment.
When toolCallCount < maxIterations, result.truncated is false and truncationReason is undefined.
When toolCallCount >= maxIterations, result.truncated is true and truncationReason contains 'Maximum iteration limit'.
result.content is the text of the final assistant message after agent_end.
Tool call errors (isError=true) emit a logger.warn that includes the tool name; they do NOT throw or abort the session.

use_cases.title: Pi tools extension registration
use_cases.description: Extension factory registers the right set of tools depending on whether an indexer URL is configured.
acceptance_criteria: Without indexerUrl, only read_file is registered.
With indexerUrl, exactly six tools are registered: read_file, search_code, find_definitions, find_usages, get_call_graph, get_impact.
Every registered tool has a non-empty label and a description longer than 20 characters.
Every tool's parameters field is a TypeBox object schema (type: 'object' with a properties map).
search_code.execute() forwards (query, repoUrl, branch, limit=10) to IndexerClient.hybridSearch and JSON-stringifies the result into content[0].text.
When IndexerClient.hybridSearch rejects, the tool's execute() rejects with the same error (no swallowing).

use_cases.title: read_file tool — path traversal guard
use_cases.description: read_file must resolve paths under repoRoot and refuse anything that escapes it.
acceptance_criteria: read_file with a relative path inside repoRoot returns the file's content as a text content block.
read_file with `../../etc/passwd` (or any path that resolves outside repoRoot) rejects with a message matching /Path traversal/.
Absolute paths that resolve outside repoRoot are also rejected.
Symlinks pointing outside repoRoot are rejected (defence in depth).

use_cases.title: Session lifecycle — timeout / abort / dispose
use_cases.description: Timeout must fire deterministically, call session.abort(), and ALWAYS dispose, even when abort() itself rejects.
acceptance_criteria: When the timeout fires, the promise rejects with a message matching /did not complete within/.
session.abort() is called exactly once on timeout.
session.dispose() is called exactly once on timeout.
If abort() throws/rejects during cleanup, the timeout error still propagates to the caller AND dispose() is still called.
extractReviewContent is called BEFORE session.dispose() (regression guard — content must be read while session.state is still valid).
session-events listener.unsubscribe() does NOT reject the `done` promise (regression guard for UnhandledPromiseRejection on Node 15+).

use_cases.title: Config schema — no legacy fields
use_cases.description: Fresh config (post-migration or first install) contains none of the dropped fields.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh config validates against the v1 Zod schema.
Fresh config does NOT contain `provider`, `model`, `variant`, or `antigravity` keys.
isLegacyConfig() returns true for any object containing the `provider` key, false otherwise.
resetConfig() writes fresh defaults after clear() (not just an empty object) — readConfig() immediately after returns a valid v1 config.

use_cases.title: Doctor command surfaces pi state
use_cases.description: `kode-review --doctor` must run even when migration is pending, and report pi installation, model availability, VCS status, and config schema version.
acceptance_criteria: --doctor runs successfully even when needsMigration() is true (i.e. is exempt from the migration gate).
Doctor output includes whether pi is installed (yes/no).
Doctor output includes whether pi has at least one usable model (yes/no).
Doctor output includes detected VCS CLI(s) — gh and/or glab.
Doctor output flags legacy config schema if present and tells the user to run --migrate or set KODE_REVIEW_MIGRATE_YES=1.

use_cases.title: show-config redacts and renders v1 schema only
use_cases.description: show-config must not leak credentials and must reflect the new schema shape.
acceptance_criteria: show-config never prints fields named `provider`, `model`, `variant`, or `antigravity` (those fields no longer exist in the schema; assertion guards against accidental re-introduction).
show-config does not print any value that looks like a token/key (any sk-…, glpat-…, gho_…, ghp_… patterns are masked or absent).
show-config exits 0 on a fresh/default config.

use_cases.title: CLI entry — migration gate ordering
use_cases.description: src/index.ts must check migration before doing any work other than --doctor.
acceptance_criteria: When needsMigration() is true and the user runs a review/setup/index command, main() emits a migration-required message and exits non-zero WITHOUT touching pi, indexer, or git.
When needsMigration() is true and the user runs --doctor, main() proceeds (doctor is exempt).
When needsMigration() is false, main() proceeds normally.
Onboarding wizard return value is honoured — if it returns false, main() exits without prompting for a review.

use_cases.title: ripgrep wrapper parses --json output
use_cases.description: The ripgrep wrapper (src/review/tools/ripgrep.ts) shells out via the project's exec helper and produces typed matches the fs tools rely on.
acceptance_criteria: parseRipgrepJsonOutput extracts {path, line, text, matchText, column} for type=match events; column is 1-based.
parseRipgrepJsonOutput ignores type=begin, type=end, type=summary, type=context events.
parseRipgrepJsonOutput returns [] for empty input.
parseRipgrepJsonOutput throws an Error containing 'parse' (case-insensitive) when given malformed JSON.
ripgrepSearch passes -F by default, -w when wholeWord=true, --type T when type='T', and -g for each entry of globs[].
ripgrepSearch treats rg exit code 1 (no matches) as success and returns [].
ripgrepSearch throws a clear error mentioning ripgrep and the install URL when rg is not on PATH.
ripgrepSearch caps results at maxResults (default 200) after parsing.

use_cases.title: git-helpers wrap log and merge-base
use_cases.description: src/review/tools/git-helpers.ts provides typed wrappers used by the agentic git tools and the dispatch layer.
acceptance_criteria: getCommitsInRange(repo, base, head) returns commits newest-first with {sha, shortSha, author, authorEmail, timestamp, subject} populated; sha is 40 hex chars.
getCommitsInRange includes a populated `body` field iff options.includeBody is true.
getCommitsInRange honours options.limit (default 50).
getCommitsInRange returns [] for an empty range (e.g. HEAD..HEAD).
getFileHistory(repo, path) returns commits that touched the named path, newest-first, capped at options.limit (default 10).
getMergeBase(repo, refA, refB) returns the 40-char SHA of the merge-base; throws when either ref is invalid.

use_cases.title: Filesystem-backed agentic tools mirror indexer output shapes
use_cases.description: Each fs-backed handler (search_code, find_definitions, find_usages, get_impact, get_call_graph) returns the same TypeScript output shape as its indexer counterpart so the dispatcher and the prompt do not branch on availability.
acceptance_criteria: searchCodeFsHandler({query}) returns SearchCodeOutput with results[].matchTypes === ['lexical'] and results.length <= 20 (MAX_LIMIT).
searchCodeFsHandler returns {results: [], totalMatches: 0} for queries with no matches; does not throw.
findDefinitionsFsHandler locates TypeScript `function`, `class`, `const`, and `interface` definitions; locates Python `def` and `class`.
findDefinitionsFsHandler returns {definitions: [], totalCount: 0} for unknown symbols; does not throw.
findUsagesFsHandler returns matches but excludes the line containing the definition keyword (function|class|def|fn|...).
findUsagesFsHandler uses whole-word matching (rg -w) so substrings of larger identifiers are not matched.
getImpactFsHandler resolves direct importers via ESM `from '...'`, CommonJS `require('...')`, and Python `from X import` patterns; sets isPartial=true and indirectImporters=[].
getImpactFsHandler sets isHighImpact=true iff directImporters.length >= 5.
getCallGraphFsHandler returns {callers: [], callees: [], available: false, reason: string} preserving the indexer schema.

use_cases.title: get_commits and get_file_history tools
use_cases.description: Two new always-on agentic tools that expose git commit metadata to the model.
acceptance_criteria: getCommitsHandler({}) uses the resolved defaultBase (merge-base with origin/HEAD or origin/main or origin/master, falling back to HEAD~20) as base, and HEAD as head.
getCommitsHandler honours input.base / input.head when provided.
getCommitsHandler caps results at limit=100 (MAX_LIMIT) and defaults to 20.
getCommitsHandler includes commit bodies iff includeBody=true.
getFileHistoryHandler returns only commits that touched the named filePath, newest-first, capped at limit=50 (MAX_LIMIT, default 10).
getFileHistoryHandler rejects path-traversal input (..) before invoking git.
Both handlers produce the documented Output shapes ({commits: CommitInfo[], totalCount: number, ...}) with no extra fields.

use_cases.title: Agentic dispatch routes per tool based on indexer + ripgrep availability
use_cases.description: The agentic tool factory (src/review/pi-tools.ts) registers tools per the table below. Replaces AC-05 behaviour for the indexer-missing case.
acceptance_criteria: When indexerUrl is set, exactly 8 tools are registered: read_file, search_code, find_definitions, find_usages, get_call_graph, get_impact, get_commits, get_file_history. The five indexer-backed tools route to their *-indexer handlers.

When indexerUrl is undefined AND rg is on PATH, the same 8 tools are registered. search_code, find_definitions, find_usages, get_impact route to their *-fs handlers. get_call_graph routes to the fs stub.
When indexerUrl is undefined AND rg is NOT on PATH, only read_file, get_call_graph (stub), get_commits, get_file_history are registered. A warning is logged naming ripgrep.
AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT lists get_commits and get_file_history alongside the original six tools, and explicitly notes that get_call_graph / get_impact may return available:false or isPartial:true.
resolveDefaultBase tries origin/HEAD, then origin/main, then origin/master, falling back to HEAD~20 — the chosen base is the merge-base of HEAD and the first reachable candidate.
Each registered tool's execute() JSON-stringifies the handler output into content[0].text — no shape change vs. existing tools.

use_cases.title: --ci flag and ci-mode helpers
use_cases.description: src/cli/args.ts adds --ci and --fail-on. src/review/ci-mode.ts owns CI-specific logic: platform detection, PR-number resolution, exit-code policy, sticky comment posting.
acceptance_criteria: --ci defaults the user's options to agentic=true, quiet=true, format='markdown', postToPr=true ONLY when those flags were not passed explicitly.
--fail-on accepts exactly 'critical' | 'high' | 'none'; any other value throws an error mentioning the allowed values.
detectCiPlatform returns 'github' iff env.GITHUB_ACTIONS === 'true'; 'gitlab' iff env.GITLAB_CI === 'true'; null otherwise.
extractPrNumber('github', env) reads /^refs\/pull\/(\d+)\// from env.GITHUB_REF; returns null when unset or non-matching.
extractPrNumber('gitlab', env) reads env.CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID as a number; returns null when unset.

resolveCiExitCode returns 0 when verdict is APPROVE regardless of fail-on; 0 when fail-on='none' regardless of counts; 1 when fail-on='critical' and critical > 0; 1 when fail-on='high' and (critical > 0 OR high > 0).
buildCommentPayload prefixes the markdown with the literal sticky marker '<!-- kode-review:sticky -->' on its own line.
postCiComment routes to githubRunner or gitlabRunner and delegates orchestration to replaceStickyComment.

When --ci is set and a PR number is resolvable (--pr or env), the CLI posts the comment; on post failure the CLI logs a warning and continues — it does not swallow the error silently and does not abort the process before exiting with the verdict-derived code.
When --ci is active the legacy --post-to-pr code path is skipped (no duplicate posts).

use_cases.title: Sticky comment replacement on PR/MR
use_cases.description: replaceStickyComment lists prior comments, identifies the ones bearing the sticky marker, posts the new comment, then deletes the priors. The orchestration is exercised against a fake CiCommentRunner.
acceptance_criteria: replaceStickyComment posts the new comment BEFORE deleting any prior comments — the PR is never left without a current review.
replaceStickyComment deletes ALL prior comments whose body contains '<!-- kode-review:sticky -->'.
replaceStickyComment does NOT delete comments that lack the sticky marker (human comments are preserved).
When the new-comment post fails, replaceStickyComment returns false and performs no deletions.
When listing prior comments throws (e.g., rate-limit), replaceStickyComment falls back to plain post and logs a warning — the new comment is still created.
When a delete fails after the post succeeded, replaceStickyComment logs a warning and returns true — partial success is reported, not retried in v1.
githubRunner.list uses `gh api --paginate repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/<pr>/comments` and selects {id, body} via --jq.
githubRunner.del uses `gh api -X DELETE repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/comments/<id>`.
gitlabRunner.list uses `glab api projects/:id/merge_requests/<iid>/notes`.
gitlabRunner.del uses `glab api -X DELETE projects/:id/merge_requests/<iid>/notes/<id>`.

use_cases.title: kode-review: ignore suppression markers
use_cases.description: Source-level magic comments tell the review to drop specific findings. The filter runs after the model returns and before the exit-code and PR-comment posting use the result. Disabled via --no-suppressions.
acceptance_criteria: hasIgnoreMarker returns true for lines containing 'kode-review: ignore' in any comment syntax (//, #, --, /* */, <!-- -->).
hasIgnoreMarker is whitespace-tolerant between the colon and the keyword ('kode-review:   ignore' matches).
hasIgnoreMarker is case-sensitive on the keyword — 'Kode-Review: ignore' does NOT match.
hasIgnoreMarker does NOT match 'kode-review: ignore-file' (that is the file-level marker, distinct).
hasIgnoreFileMarker returns true when any line in the file contains 'kode-review: ignore-file'.
filterSuppressedFindings drops a finding when the referenced line carries the ignore marker.
filterSuppressedFindings drops a finding when the line IMMEDIATELY ABOVE the referenced line carries the ignore marker (next-line idiom).
filterSuppressedFindings drops every finding in a file containing the ignore-file marker.
filterSuppressedFindings keeps findings when the referenced file cannot be read (deleted, permission denied) — never silently drop on error.
filterSuppressedFindings keeps findings when the referenced path attempts traversal (e.g. '../../etc/passwd') — the path guard rejects access, the finding survives.
filterSuppressedFindings rewrites the 'Issues Summary: X CRITICAL, Y HIGH, ...' line to reflect the new counts.
filterSuppressedFindings appends a 'Suppressed: N finding(s) via `kode-review: ignore` markers.' footer when N > 0.
The CLI runs the filter ONLY when options.noSuppressions is false (default). With --no-suppressions, the raw review output is used unchanged.
The CLI runs the filter BEFORE counting severities and posting the comment — suppressed findings affect neither the exit code nor the PR comment.
AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT instructs the model to honor 'kode-review: ignore' and 'kode-review: ignore-file' markers so suppressed findings ideally never appear in the first place (defense-in-depth).

use_cases.title: Repo-scope install gate — clawpatch on PATH + Node 22 check
use_cases.description: Before --scope repo runs anything that needs clawpatch, the orchestrator must verify `clawpatch` is installed and the host Node major is at least clawpatch's minimum, and otherwise print a package-manager-aware install hint or a Node-upgrade hint.
acceptance_criteria: detectClawpatch() returns { installed: false, version: null } when `clawpatch` is not on PATH (commandExists returns false).
detectClawpatch() returns { installed: true, version: <string> } when `clawpatch --version` exits 0 with non-empty stdout.
detectClawpatch() returns { installed: true, version: null } when `clawpatch` exists on PATH but `clawpatch --version` exits non-zero or prints empty output (installed-but-unhealthy).
isNodeVersionCompatible() returns true iff the running Node major version parsed from process.version is >= 22 (CLAWPATCH_MIN_NODE_MAJOR).
detectPreferredPackageManager(repoRoot) returns 'bun' when `bun.lock` or `bun.lockb` exists, 'pnpm' when `pnpm-lock.yaml` exists, 'yarn' when `yarn.lock` exists, and 'npm' otherwise.

buildInstallHint(repoRoot) lists the preferred package manager's install command first (e.g. `bun add -g clawpatch`, `pnpm add -g clawpatch`, `yarn global add clawpatch`, or `npm install -g clawpatch`) and always includes the `npx clawpatch <command>` ephemeral fallback line.
buildInstallHint(repoRoot) appends `npm install -g clawpatch` as a secondary line whenever the preferred manager is not npm.
buildInstallHint(repoRoot) ends with a `(Requires Node.js >= 22)` line.
buildNodeUpgradeHint() includes the literal string 'requires Node.js >= 22' and the current `process.version` value.
runRepoAudit() throws an Error whose message equals buildNodeUpgradeHint() when isNodeVersionCompatible() returns false, before any clawpatch shell-out or feature read occurs.
runRepoAudit() throws an Error whose message equals buildInstallHint(repoRoot) when detectClawpatch() returns installed=false and cli.reportOnly is false.
runRepoAudit() does NOT call detectClawpatch() when cli.reportOnly is true — report-only mode reads `.kode-review/findings/` directly with no install gate.

use_cases.title: clawpatch CLI wrappers — map / doctor / passthrough
use_cases.description: src/repo-audit/clawpatch-cli.ts wraps the external `clawpatch` binary via the project's execa-based runCommand (no shell) for the `map`, `doctor`, and generic passthrough operations the orchestrator needs.
acceptance_criteria: runClawpatchMap(repoRoot) invokes `clawpatch map` with cwd set to repoRoot and returns { exitCode, stdout, stderr } from the underlying runCommand.
runClawpatchMap(repoRoot, { force: true }) appends `--force` as the second argv token (after `map`).
runClawpatchMap(repoRoot, { force: false }) does NOT append `--force`.
runClawpatchDoctor(repoRoot) invokes `clawpatch doctor --json` with cwd set to repoRoot.
runClawpatchDoctor(repoRoot, { provider, model }) appends `--provider <provider>` and/or `--model <model>` when those options are defined and omits them when undefined.
runClawpatchDoctor() returns payload=<parsed JSON> when stdout parses as JSON, and payload=null when stdout is not valid JSON (the binary may be broken).
runClawpatch(repoRoot, args) invokes `clawpatch <args...>` verbatim and returns the same { exitCode, stdout, stderr } shape.
runRepoAudit() throws an Error containing 'clawpatch map failed' and the captured stderr when runClawpatchMap returns a non-zero exitCode.
runRepoAudit() forwards cli.remap to runClawpatchMap as { force: cli.remap } — `--remap` on the CLI translates to `--force` on `clawpatch map`.

use_cases.title: Feature record reading — read-only consumer of .clawpatch/features/
use_cases.description: src/repo-audit/features.ts reads `.clawpatch/features/*.json` into validated FeatureRecord[] and never writes to `.clawpatch/`. Malformed files are surfaced as warnings, not fatal errors.
acceptance_criteria: clawpatchFeaturesDir(repoRoot) returns `<repoRoot>/.clawpatch/features` using path.join (CLAWPATCH_STATE_DIR='.clawpatch', CLAWPATCH_FEATURES_DIR='features').
readFeatures(repoRoot) returns { features: [], skipped: [] } when `.clawpatch/features/` does not exist (ENOENT), so callers can treat this as 'no features mapped yet'.
readFeatures(repoRoot) rethrows readdir errors whose code is anything other than 'ENOENT'.
readFeatures(repoRoot) ignores non-`.json` entries in the directory.
readFeatures(repoRoot) records files with invalid JSON in `skipped` with a 'invalid JSON: ...' reason and emits a logger.warn naming the path — it does NOT throw.
readFeatures(repoRoot) records files that fail FeatureRecordSchema validation in `skipped` with the Zod issue messages joined by '; ' and emits a logger.warn — it does NOT throw.

readFeatures(repoRoot) accepts FeatureRecord JSON whose `schemaVersion` differs from SUPPORTED_FEATURE_SCHEMA_VERSION (=1) but emits a logger.warn telling the user to upgrade kode-review; the record is still returned in `features`.
readFeatures(repoRoot) returns features sorted by `featureId` (a.featureId.localeCompare(b.featureId)) so iteration order is deterministic across runs.
FeatureRecordSchema rejects payloads missing `featureId`, `title`, `summary`, `kind`, `source`, `confidence`, `status`, `createdAt`, or `updatedAt`.
FeatureRecordSchema defaults `entrypoints`, `ownedFiles`, `contextFiles`, `tests`, `tags`, and `trustBoundaries` to [] when absent in the source JSON (forward-compat cushion).

FeatureRecordSchema.kind is constrained to the FEATURE_KINDS enum (12 values including 'cli-command', 'route', 'ui-flow', 'service', 'job', 'agent-tool', 'library', 'config', 'release', 'test-suite', 'infra', 'unknown').

pendingFeatures(features) returns features whose status is 'pending' as-is; for status === 'error' it emits a logger.warn naming the featureId and still includes the feature in the result (retry-on-rerun policy); features in any other status are excluded.
No code path in src/repo-audit/features.ts (or any other src/repo-audit/* module) writes inside `.clawpatch/`.

use_cases.title: Persona dispatch — trust boundaries + kind → reviewer set
use_cases.description: selectPersonas(feature) maps a FeatureRecord's `trustBoundaries` and `kind` to the built-in reviewer set in a stable order. resolvePersonasWithOverride layers an explicit `--reviewer` override on top.
acceptance_criteria: selectPersonas always includes 'general' as the first element of the returned array.
selectPersonas appends 'architect' when feature.kind is 'library' or 'service' (ARCHITECT_KINDS) and not for any other kind.
selectPersonas appends 'security' when feature.trustBoundaries contains any of: 'user-input', 'network', 'serialization', 'external-api', 'auth', 'permissions', 'secrets' (SECURITY_BOUNDARIES).
selectPersonas does NOT append 'security' for features whose only trust boundaries are 'filesystem', 'database', 'process-exec', or 'concurrency' alone — those are not auto-triggers.
selectPersonas appends 'test-auditor' when feature.kind === 'test-suite' OR feature.tests.length > 0.
selectPersonas never auto-includes 'doc-reviewer' — doc review must be requested explicitly via `--reviewer doc-reviewer`.
selectPersonas returns personas in the fixed order: general → architect → security → test-auditor (matching the order downstream rendering uses to group findings).
resolvePersonasWithOverride(feature, []) returns selectPersonas(feature) verbatim — an empty override means auto-dispatch.
resolvePersonasWithOverride(feature, ['security','general','security']) returns ['security','general'] — the override is used verbatim, deduplicated, and order-preserving.

The orchestrator's call site treats cli.reviewers === undefined OR cli.reviewers deeply-equals ['general'] (the CLI default) as 'no override' — in those cases the override array passed to resolvePersonasWithOverride is [].

use_cases.title: Feature-review prompt construction with file caps
use_cases.description: buildFeatureReviewPrompt produces a (systemSuffix, userPrompt) pair that re-targets the persona's anti-hallucination rules from 'the diff' to 'owned/context files in this prompt or reachable via tools', and inlines up to MAX_OWNED_FILES_IN_PROMPT owned files and MAX_CONTEXT_FILES_IN_PROMPT context files. Files past the cap are referenced by path so the agent can fetch them with read_file.
acceptance_criteria: REPO_AUDIT_DEFAULTS.MAX_OWNED_FILES_IN_PROMPT equals 12.
REPO_AUDIT_DEFAULTS.MAX_CONTEXT_FILES_IN_PROMPT equals 24.
FEATURE_REVIEW_MODE_SUFFIX begins with the literal heading '## FEATURE REVIEW MODE (added by --scope repo)'.
buildFeatureReviewPrompt returns systemSuffix === FEATURE_REVIEW_MODE_SUFFIX verbatim.
buildFeatureReviewPrompt's userPrompt contains a '<feature_metadata>' block with the feature's featureId, title, kind, confidence, and summary.
buildFeatureReviewPrompt's userPrompt emits a 'trust_boundaries:' line iff feature.trustBoundaries.length > 0; emits a 'tags:' line iff feature.tags.length > 0.

buildFeatureReviewPrompt inlines the first maxOwnedFiles (default 12) of feature.ownedFiles inside `<file path="..." reason="...">` blocks with a fenced code block whose language hint is derived from the file extension via langHintFromPath; remaining owned files appear under a '### Additional owned files (use read_file to view)' header as `- <path>  (<reason>)` bullets.
buildFeatureReviewPrompt inlines the first maxContextFiles (default 24) of feature.contextFiles the same way and lists the remainder under '### Additional context files (use read_file to view)'.
buildFeatureReviewPrompt populates `inlinedFiles` with the paths whose bodies were embedded and `deferredFiles` with the paths that were over the cap or whose readFileSafe returned null.

When readFileSafe returns null for an owned/context file (missing, traversal-rejected), buildFeatureReviewPrompt emits a self-closing `<file ... deferred="true" />` placeholder and adds the path to deferredFiles — it does NOT throw and does NOT inline a stale body.
buildFeatureReviewPrompt emits a '## Tests' section with a `<tests>` block iff feature.tests.length > 0.
buildFeatureReviewPrompt ends with a '## Output Instructions' section whose body names the per-feature cap from REPO_AUDIT_DEFAULTS.MAX_FINDINGS_PER_FEATURE (=10).
langHintFromPath maps 'ts'→'typescript', 'tsx'→'tsx', 'py'→'python', 'go'→'go', 'rs'→'rust', and returns '' for unknown extensions.

use_cases.title: kode-agent engine wiring — runAgenticReview honors systemPrompt + userPromptOverride
use_cases.description: reviewFeatureWithAgent builds the feature prompt, composes the persona system prompt with FEATURE_REVIEW_MODE_SUFFIX, and calls the shared runAgenticReview loop with overrides so the same agent path powers both diff-scope and repo-scope reviews. Findings are capped at MAX_FINDINGS_PER_FEATURE.
acceptance_criteria: reviewFeatureWithAgent calls buildFeatureReviewPrompt({ feature, repoRoot }) and forwards the resulting userPrompt as runAgenticReview's `userPromptOverride`.
reviewFeatureWithAgent composes systemPrompt as `loadReviewerSystemPrompt(persona) + '\n\n' + built.systemSuffix` and passes it as runAgenticReview's `systemPrompt`.

reviewFeatureWithAgent passes the placeholder diffContent='' and context=`repo-scope feature review: <featureId>` to runAgenticReview (the diff-mode inputs are unused when userPromptOverride is set, but the runAgenticReview signature requires them).
reviewFeatureWithAgent forwards repoRoot, repoUrl, branch, indexerUrl, model, maxIterations, and timeoutSec (as `timeout`) to runAgenticReview verbatim.
runAgenticReview's prompt resolution uses options.userPromptOverride as the user prompt when defined, falling back to buildAgenticPrompt otherwise (engine.ts line ~304).
runAgenticReview's system prompt resolution uses options.systemPrompt when defined, falling back to AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT otherwise (engine.ts line ~321).

runReview (non-agentic) also honors options.systemPrompt (passed as systemPromptOverride to the underlying session) and options.userPromptOverride (engine.ts lines ~255 and ~274) — the override surface is shared by both engines.

reviewFeatureWithAgent caps the returned findings at REPO_AUDIT_DEFAULTS.MAX_FINDINGS_PER_FEATURE (=10) via result.findings.slice(0, cap) — defense in depth against a chatty model that ignores the prompt's cap.

reviewFeatureWithAgent returns a ReviewFeatureResult containing feature, persona, capped findings, raw content, usage, truncated, and truncationReason — preserving the truncation flag from runAgenticReview.

use_cases.title: State persistence — atomic finding writes, O_EXCL locks with stale reclamation, run history
use_cases.description: src/repo-audit/state.ts owns `.kode-review/`. Findings are written via temp-write-rename so partial files are never observed. Feature locks use O_EXCL create with a TOCTOU-safe rename-over-stale reclamation path. Run history is appended one JSON line at a time.
acceptance_criteria: stateDir(repoRoot), findingsDir(repoRoot), and locksDir(repoRoot) resolve to `<repoRoot>/.kode-review`, `<repoRoot>/.kode-review/findings`, and `<repoRoot>/.kode-review/locks` respectively (STATE_DIR_NAME='.kode-review', FINDINGS_DIR='findings', LOCKS_DIR='locks').
ensureStateDirs(repoRoot) creates both findings/ and locks/ with `{ recursive: true }` and is idempotent.
resetState(repoRoot) removes the entire `.kode-review/` tree with `{ recursive: true, force: true }`.

computeFindingId(featureId, file, lineStart, title) returns the first 24 hex chars of sha1(`${featureId}\0${file}\0${lineStart}\0${title}`) — stable across runs and not dependent on time or random input.

writeFinding(repoRoot, record) writes JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2) + '\n' to a temp file named `<path>.tmp.<pid>.<8-hex>` and renames it over `<findingId>.json` so partial files are never visible to readers.
readFinding(repoRoot, findingId) returns null when the file does not exist (ENOENT) and parses with RepoFindingRecordSchema otherwise.
listFindings(repoRoot) returns [] when findings/ does not exist (ENOENT), skips non-`.json` entries, and logs a warn for files that fail to parse without throwing.
acquireFeatureLock(repoRoot, featureId, runId) first attempts writeFile with `flag: 'wx'` (O_EXCL create) and returns LockInfo on success.
acquireFeatureLock returns null when the lock file exists and its mtime is newer than KODE_REVIEW_LOCK_STALE_MS (defaults to 30 minutes = 30*60*1000 ms).

acquireFeatureLock atomically reclaims a stale lock by writing to `<path>.steal.<pid>.<8-hex>` and renaming it over the lock path — two concurrent reclamation attempts cannot both succeed because POSIX rename is atomic.
acquireFeatureLock honors the KODE_REVIEW_LOCK_STALE_MS environment variable when it parses to a positive integer; non-numeric or non-positive values fall back to the 30-minute default.
releaseFeatureLock(repoRoot, featureId) unlinks the lock file and swallows any error (best-effort cleanup).

Feature ids that contain filesystem-unsafe characters are encoded for the lock filename via encodeFeatureIdForFs: replace each non-[A-Za-z0-9._-] character with '_', truncate to 200 chars, then append a `.` and the first 8 hex chars of sha1(featureId) — so two distinct long ids cannot collide on the truncated prefix.

appendRunHistory(repoRoot, entry) appends `JSON.stringify(entry) + '\n'` to `<stateDir>/run-history.jsonl` using `flag: 'a'`; the orchestrator records one entry per run with runId, startedAt, endedAt, engine, featuresReviewed, findingsEmitted, and (when present) model + since.
newRunId() returns a string starting with the literal prefix 'run-' followed by Date.now() and an 8-hex slice of randomUUID().

use_cases.title: Structured suppression filter for Finding[] arrays
use_cases.description: filterSuppressedStructured drops findings whose source location is annotated with `kode-review: ignore` or `kode-review: ignore-file`. It shares readFileSafe and shouldSuppressAtLine with the diff-scope filter so the two implementations cannot drift, and caches per-file reads within a single call.
acceptance_criteria: filterSuppressedStructured(findings, repoRoot) drops a finding when shouldSuppressAtLine(content, finding.lineStart) returns true for the finding's file.
filterSuppressedStructured caches readFileSafe results in a per-call Map keyed by finding.file — the same file is read at most once per invocation.

filterSuppressedStructured keeps a finding whose file cannot be read (readFileSafe returns null due to deletion, permission error, or path-traversal rejection) — never silently drop on file-access failure.
filterSuppressedStructured returns { kept, suppressedCount, byFile } where byFile is a Map<string, number> of per-file suppression counts for logging.

filterSuppressedStructured imports readFileSafe and shouldSuppressAtLine from '../review/suppressions.js' — the marker grammar and path-guarded read are shared with the diff-scope filter so behavior cannot drift.
The orchestrator runs filterSuppressedStructured only when cli.noSuppressions is false; with --no-suppressions, kept = result.findings verbatim and totalSuppressed = 0.

The orchestrator's per-feature suppression count is added to RunRepoAuditResult.findingsSuppressed and logged in yellow with the literal phrase 'kode-review: ignore markers' when suppressedThisRun > 0.

use_cases.title: --since <ref> feature filter via three-dot git diff
use_cases.description: filterFeaturesBySince reduces the feature set to those whose `ownedFiles[].path` intersects the files changed in `<ref>...HEAD`. Context files are deliberately not used for matching — context is a hint, not a re-review trigger.
acceptance_criteria: touchedFilesSince(repoRoot, ref) invokes `git diff --name-only <ref>...HEAD` with cwd=repoRoot via the project's runCommand wrapper (no shell).

touchedFilesSince throws an Error containing 'git diff --name-only <ref>...HEAD failed' and the captured stderr when git exits non-zero — callers must surface this, not silently fall back to 'all features'.
touchedFilesSince splits stdout on '\n', trims each line, and filters out empty strings to produce the touched-file list.
filterFeaturesBySince returns { matched: [], touchedFiles: [] } when git reports zero touched files.
filterFeaturesBySince returns only features that have at least one ownedFile whose `path` is in the touched-file Set; features matched purely on contextFiles are excluded.
Features with zero ownedFiles never match — they have no surface to touch under the ownership rule.
The orchestrator applies the --since filter BEFORE the 'already reviewed' filter so users can combine `--since <ref>` with `--remap` to re-review touched features.
The orchestrator logs the literal pattern `--since <ref>: <N> file(s) changed, <M> feature(s) match` after running the filter.

use_cases.title: Repo-audit report renderer — text / markdown / json with Feature × Severity matrix
use_cases.description: renderRepoReport formats RepoFindingRecord[] in text, markdown, or json. text and markdown emit a Feature × Severity matrix sorted by CRITICAL count, then HIGH, then total — so reviewers can see at-a-glance which feature is in worst shape.
acceptance_criteria: renderRepoReport({ records, format: 'json' }) returns a JSON string whose root has `version: 1`, `generatedAt`, `total`, `byStatus`, `bySeverity`, and `findings[]` fields.
The JSON `bySeverity` rollup counts only records whose `status === 'open'`; closed findings are excluded from the severity tally.

Each `findings[]` entry in the JSON projection contains findingId, featureId, persona, status, severity, category, confidence, title, file, lineStart, lineEnd, evidence, problem, recommendation, createdByRunId, createdAt, updatedAt — and no other fields.
renderRepoReport({ format: 'text' }) emits the literal header line '  Repo Audit Report' between two box-drawing rule lines.

Text and markdown headers include 'Total findings', 'Open', and 'Closed' counters; when suppressionsDisabled is true, they also include 'Suppressions: DISABLED (--no-suppressions)' (text) or '`--no-suppressions`' (markdown).

When records.length === 0, text output ends with 'No findings on disk. Run without --report-only to produce a review.' and markdown ends with '_No findings on disk. Run without `--report-only` to produce a review._' — and neither emits a Feature × Severity matrix.
Text renderer emits an 'Open by severity:' rollup listing all four SEVERITIES (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) with their counts, in that fixed order.
buildFeatureRows sorts features by CRITICAL count desc, then HIGH desc, then total desc — the worst-impacted features always appear first in the matrix.
formatFeatureSeverityTable renders the matrix with column widths sized to the longest featureId and a `feature | CRIT | HIGH | MED | LOW | TOTAL` header.
formatFeatureSeverityMarkdownTable renders the matrix as a markdown table with right-aligned numeric columns (`---------:` separator).
writeRepoReport({ outputFile, quiet }) writes the rendered content to outputFile (mkdir -p its directory first) and additionally prints to stdout iff quiet is not true.

use_cases.title: Repo-audit orchestrator pipeline — install gate → map → filter → review → persist
use_cases.description: runRepoAudit ties install detection, clawpatch mapping, feature reading, since-filtering, already-reviewed skipping, persona dispatch, kode-agent review, structured suppression, finding persistence, and run-history recording into one pipeline. --report-only short-circuits after install gate; --revalidate is rejected with a clear message until its sibling path lands.
acceptance_criteria: runRepoAudit throws an Error containing '--revalidate is not yet implemented in this build' when cli.revalidate is true — never silently runs a full audit in its place.

runRepoAudit returns { featuresReviewed: 0, featuresSkipped: 0, findingsEmitted: 0, findingsSuppressed: 0, findingsOnDisk: <listFindings count> } and never calls runClawpatchMap when cli.reportOnly is true.
runRepoAudit passes { force: cli.remap } to runClawpatchMap — `--remap` is the only path that forces a re-map.
runRepoAudit short-circuits with featuresReviewed=0 and a 'clawpatch produced no features for this repo' warn when readFeatures returns an empty features array.
runRepoAudit emits a 'Skipped <N> malformed feature file(s).' warn when readFeatures.skipped is non-empty.
runRepoAudit builds the 'already reviewed' set with a single listFindings() call (not per-candidate hasFindingsForFeature) to keep startup O(N+M) rather than O(N*M).
runRepoAudit's already-reviewed filter is bypassed (set is empty) when cli.remap is true — a remap may have re-shaped features, so prior findings are treated as stale.

runRepoAudit reviews each pending feature once per persona returned by resolvePersonasWithOverride; for each finding it writes a RepoFindingRecord with schemaVersion=1, status='open', createdByRunId=<runId>, and createdAt/updatedAt set to the current ISO timestamp.
runRepoAudit writes each finding via writeFinding (atomic temp-write-rename through state.ts) — never bypasses the atomic write path.

runRepoAudit appends exactly one run-history entry per invocation via appendRunHistory with engine='kode-agent', the runId from newRunId(), startedAt set before the review loop, endedAt set after, plus featuresReviewed, findingsEmitted, model, and since.
runRepoAudit logs a yellow warn 'Suppressed <N> finding(s) via kode-review: ignore markers' once per persona pass when suppressedThisRun > 0.
runRepoAudit logs a warn including the persona name when reviewFeatureWithAgent returns truncated=true.

Module documentation and code enforce the state boundary: src/repo-audit/* writes only into `.kode-review/` and never into `.clawpatch/`; clawpatch owns `.clawpatch/` and is treated as read-only by features.ts.

use_cases.title: CLI surface for --scope repo — engine / remap / jobs / since / report-only / revalidate / clawpatch-compat
use_cases.description: src/cli/args.ts wires the repo-scope flags into CliOptions, validates them, and rejects incoherent combinations (--scope repo + --pr; --report-only + --revalidate).
acceptance_criteria: ReviewScope includes the literal 'repo'; createProgram registers `-s, --scope <scope>` with valid values 'local', 'pr', 'both', 'auto', 'repo' and default 'auto'.
createProgram registers --engine, --remap, --jobs, --since, --report-only, --revalidate, and --clawpatch-compat under the 'Repo-scope audit' option group.
--engine defaults to 'kode-agent'; parseArgs throws an Error containing 'Invalid --engine' when the value is anything other than 'kode-agent' or 'clawpatch'.
--jobs defaults to '4'; parseArgs throws an Error containing 'Invalid --jobs' when the parsed integer is NaN, < 1, or > 32.
--remap, --report-only, --revalidate, and --clawpatch-compat each default to false in the returned CliOptions when the flag is not passed.
--since stores the raw ref string in CliOptions.since (no validation at parse time — git resolves it at run time in touchedFilesSince).
parseArgs throws an Error containing '--scope repo cannot be combined with --pr' when opts.scope === 'repo' and opts.pr is set (PR scope is diff-scoped and incompatible with whole-repo scope).
parseArgs throws an Error containing '--report-only and --revalidate cannot be combined' when both flags are true — their intents are contradictory (skip all model calls vs. make them).
The help footer mentions `kode-review -s repo`, `kode-review -s repo --since main`, and `kode-review -s repo --report-only` so users discover the repo-scope surface.
